title: Phillyrea lanceolata
taxonomy: Phillyrea lanceolata is a member of the family Oleaceae, commonly known as the olive family. This family is a group of flowering plants that includes other species such as forsythia and jasmine. Phillyrea lanceolata is native to the Mediterranean region and is a shrub or small tree with a broad, rounded crown. It is classified as a species within the genus Phillyrea, which includes two other species. The exact classification of Phillyrea lanceolata is Phillyrea lanceolata, a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae.
uses: Phillyrea lanceolata is used in traditional medicine, primarily in the Mediterranean region, to treat various health issues.
